target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2D Slack opt-in broker routing proof
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Slack DMs and channel threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Slack remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2D - Slack opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. - Head: `7614f3bcfb` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `7614f3bcfb` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2D Slack migration proof for the Channel Broker rollout. - Keeps native Slack default behavior unchanged; this only proves opt-in broker routing for Slack targets. - Stacks on Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86112 Related #86108 Related #86105 Supersedes closed PR #86142, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack DM targets such as `broker:slack:user:U12345678` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "direct"`. - Normalize broker-prefixed Slack channel thread targets such as `broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001` into broker provider requests with `conversation.type = "channel"` and `threadId`. - Canonicalize Slack broker session routes to the resolved platform target, preserving direct-route semantics as `slack:direct%3A` and thread routes as `slack:?threadId=`. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Slack DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and Slack `threadTs` as broker `threadId`. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:slack:channel:C12345678?threadId=1716500000.000001"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "slack-thread", "messageId": "native-slack-thread", "platform": "slack", "conversation": { "id": "C12345678", "type": "channel", "threadId": "1716500000.000001" }, "requirements": { "text": true, "thread": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced the expected Slack provider request and kept threaded delivery represented by the broker `threadId` field. - **What was not tested:** Live Slack API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - No native Slack migration is enabled by default in this PR.

Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR proves Discord DMs, channels, and threads can route through broker-owned semantics while native Discord remains the default path. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2C - Discord opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. - Head: `56bc4818a1` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `56bc4818a1` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2C Discord broker routing proof on top of #86154, #86153, and #86096. - Normalizes broker-routed Discord DMs and channel threads without importing native Discord extension internals. - Preserves non-channel conversation type in canonical broker routes so Discord DMs remain direct routes. ## Rollout Context Closes #86111 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86154 Stacked on #86153 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86139, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. Native Discord defaults stay unchanged. Existing prefix ownership still keeps `discord:` owned by the native Discord plugin when installed; this PR proves the opt-in broker route through `broker:discord:...` and the broker alias path when native Discord is absent. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Discord DM and threaded-channel targets can route through `channel-broker` while preserving direct/channel conversation semantics and provider-owned ids. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:discord:user:123456789012345678"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "discord-dm", "messageId": "native-discord-dm", "platform": "discord", "conversation": { "id": "123456789012345678", "type": "direct" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path produced a direct Discord broker provider request rather than an ambiguous channel-looking target. - **What was not tested:** Live Discord API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. ### Behavior OpenClaw can represent Discord DMs and channel threads through `channel-broker` using stable broker semantics: provider platform `discord`, direct/channel conversation type, conversation id, and optional thread id. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-discord-routing` - Head: `56bc4818a1` (current Discord proof head) ### Steps 1. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:user:123456789012345678`. 2. Verified the provider request used `platform: "discord"`, `conversation.type: "direct"`, and `conversation.id: "123456789012345678"`. 3. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:discord:channel:222222222222222222?threadId=333333333333333333`. 4. Verified the provider request used channel conversation id plus `threadId`. 5. Resolved the outbound session route for the Discord DM target. 6. Verified the route stayed direct and canonicalized to `discord:direct%3A123456789012345678`. ### Evidence - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 2 files passed, 13 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result Discord DMs and threads now flow through broker target parsing and provider request semantics, not through core or native Discord extension branches. ### Not Tested - Live Discord API traffic; this PR is the opt-in broker migration proof, not a live provider connector. - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2B Telegram broker routing proof on top of #86153 and #86096. - Normalizes Telegram topic shorthand in the broker plugin, including `broker:telegram::topic:` and numeric `:` targets. - Canonicalizes outbound broker session routes to `telegram:?threadId=` so topic routing is stable without importing native Telegram internals. ## Rollout Context Closes #86110 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86153 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86137, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. Native Telegram defaults stay unchanged. Existing target-prefix ownership still keeps `telegram:` owned by the native Telegram plugin when that plugin is installed; this PR proves the opt-in broker route through `broker:telegram:...` and the broker alias path when native Telegram is absent. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram topic targets can route through `channel-broker` and become provider requests with canonical `telegram` platform, channel conversation id, and topic `threadId`. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:telegram:-1001234567890:topic:42"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "telegram-topic", "messageId": "native-telegram-topic", "platform": "telegram", "conversation": { "id": "-1001234567890", "type": "channel", "threadId": "42" }, "requirements": { "text": true, "thread": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path normalized Telegram topic shorthand into the broker provider request shape without importing native Telegram extension internals. - **What was not tested:** Live Telegram Bot API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. ### Behavior OpenClaw can route a Telegram topic through `channel-broker` without Telegram extension code. The broker receives a normalized platform `telegram`, conversation id, and thread id; OpenClaw keeps the session route canonical and leaves native Telegram prefix ownership intact. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing` - Head: `d1de4ad7c8` (current Telegram proof head) ### Steps 1. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:telegram:-1001234567890:topic:42` through `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text`. 2. Verified the provider request used `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.id: "-1001234567890"`, and `conversation.threadId: "42"`. 3. Resolved the outbound session route for the same target. 4. Verified the route canonicalized to `telegram:-1001234567890?threadId=42` and preserved channel chat semantics. ### Evidence - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 2 files passed, 10 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result Telegram topic routing is represented by broker-owned target normalization and provider request semantics, not by core importing or calling native Telegram extension internals. ### Not Tested - Live Telegram Bot API traffic; this PR is the opt-in broker migration proof, not a live provider connector. - Full local suite. target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2A conformance harness
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the regression shield. It turns the recurring channel pain into shared conformance: signed inbound webhooks, normalization, dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2A - Conformance and signed inbound baseline - Role: Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff, payload/live-preview delivery requests, /verbose channelData preservation, and explicit listed-provider gating. - Head: `3f295c3742` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `3f295c3742` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2A Channel Broker conformance baseline on top of #86096. - Extends `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` with inbound normalization, dedupe-key, provider capability normalization, and platform capability checks. - Adds bundled broker conformance coverage for durable inbound receive, durable final send receipts, retryable receipt failures, preview/progress capability gates, and `/verbose`/tool-message `channelData`. ## Rollout Context Closes #86109 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86133, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. This PR intentionally does not migrate Telegram, Discord, or Slack routing. Native defaults stay unchanged until the Phase 2B/2C/2D proofs land behind opt-in broker routing. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** The broker conformance baseline normalizes inbound events, builds durable dedupe keys, evaluates preview/progress capabilities, and sends outbound requests through the bundled broker lifecycle without platform-specific core branches. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that imported `src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts`, created a Telegram inbound event, built the durable dedupe key, evaluated Discord preview/progress capabilities, and sent broker outbound requests through `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "inboundDedupeKey": "acme:bot-main:telegram:live-proof-telegram-1", "previewProgressSupported": true } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path produced the expected durable inbound key and capability decision outside the Vitest harness, proving the Phase 2A contract surface can carry the normalized lifecycle facts that later channel migrations consume. - **What was not tested:** Live provider network traffic and the full local suite; those remain covered by later provider proofs and GitHub Actions. ### Behavior The broker contract now has a conformance harness for the lifecycle paths that regress most often across maintained chat channels: inbound normalization/dedupe, durable final sends, provider receipts, retry/failure propagation, preview/progress capability checks, and `/verbose` tool-message metadata. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-conformance` - Head: `3f295c3742` (current Phase 2A head) ### Steps 1. Normalized a synthetic Telegram inbound event through the SDK and accepted it through the durable inbound receive journal. 2. Replayed the same dedupe key while pending and after completion to prove duplicate/redelivery handling. 3. Sent a synthetic Slack final message through the bundled broker runtime and verified provider receipt commit material. 4. Returned a retryable Discord provider receipt and verified the send fails with structured receipt context. 5. Built preview/finalize broker requests and asserted Discord preview/progress support through declared capabilities. 6. Passed `/verbose` tool-message policy through broker `channelData` without platform-specific branching. ### Evidence - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - 1 file passed, 7 tests passed. - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 3 files passed, 10 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result The SDK helpers normalize provider payloads without dropping native metadata, the bundled plugin refuses non-sent provider receipts as durable-send failures, and conformance tests prove the current broker path can represent the Phase 2 lifecycle requirements without changing user-visible channel defaults. ### Not Tested - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. - Live Telegram, Discord, or Slack provider traffic. Those are intentionally scoped to Phase 2B/2C/2D. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 1 - SDK and bundled broker foundation - Role: Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. - Head: `483c5b80e2` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `483c5b80e2` Focused local validation after the latest Phase 1 package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts --maxWorkers=1` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary Adds Phase 1 of the Channel Broker consolidation program: a versioned `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol surface, a bundled `channel-broker` channel plugin, provider-owned outbound request/receipt helpers, docs, and focused tests. This PR intentionally stays at the protocol/foundation layer. Telegram, Discord, Slack, and later channel migrations are tracked separately and should not be folded into this PR. ## Linked Context Closes #86107 Related #86105 Related #86108 Related #86109 Related #86110 Related #86111 Related #86112 Related #86113 Related #86114 Related #86115 ## Review Repair History The current Phase 1 branch includes focused repairs for ClawSweeper's blocker classes: - Preserves native prefix ownership when `channel-broker` declares platform aliases that overlap native plugins. If Telegram/Discord/Slack are registered, their prefixes stay native-owned; broker aliases own a platform prefix only when no native plugin declares it. - Adds the standard channel SecretRef contract for `channels.channel-broker.{outboundToken,signingSecret}` plus `accounts.*` and `providers.*` variants, and changes the broker schema/docs to use SecretRef-compatible credential input. - Threads `AbortSignal` through broker outbound requests, including the HTTP `fetch` transport and custom runtime override path. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Verified the Phase 1 broker surface resolves as a bundled channel and produces a normalized V1 outbound request for a Telegram-scoped broker target. This proves the user-visible foundation being shipped in this PR: catalog discovery, channel id normalization, target construction, protocol versioning, platform normalization, thread preservation, and final-send payload shape. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, after the current Phase 1 repair/doc series through `d39d47b3db`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); const target = buildBrokerConversationTarget({ platform: "Telegram", conversationId: "chat 123", threadId: "topic-7" }); const request = createBrokerOutboundRequest({ requestId: "send-proof-1", providerId: "synthetic-provider", platform: "Telegram", accountId: "default", conversation: { id: "chat 123", type: "channel", threadId: "topic-7" }, mode: "final", payloads: [{ text: "broker proof" }], requirements: { text: true, thread: true } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));' ``` - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above: ```json { "discoveredChannel": { "id": "channel-broker", "order": 60, "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker" }, "channelNormalization": "channel-broker", "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7", "outboundProtocolVersion": 1, "outboundMode": "final", "outboundPlatform": "telegram", "outboundConversation": { "id": "chat 123", "type": "channel", "threadId": "topic-7" }, "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof" } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** OpenClaw discovered the bundled `channel-broker` entry, normalized the channel id, built a Telegram broker target that preserved `threadId`, and emitted a V1 final outbound request with normalized `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.threadId: "topic-7"`, and payload text `broker proof`. - **What was not tested:** No real third-party broker provider delivery was tested in Phase 1. That is intentionally deferred to the Phase 2 migration proof issues for Telegram, Discord, and Slack. ## Focused Local Validation All local validation below ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: ```bash git diff --check ``` ```bash node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/runtime.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ## Known Inherited Gates - `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check` has an inherited clean-upstream SDK budget drift; this PR adds the intended `plugin-sdk/channel-broker` public subpath. - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` has inherited unrelated glossary-label failures on clean upstream; Channel Broker glossary entries were added in this branch.

target.title: fix(agents): strip plaintext model provider keys
target.body:
## Summary
- strip plaintext provider `apiKey` values after merge-mode preservation, source-managed enforcement, and provider compatibility transforms so old `models.json` keys cannot be reintroduced into the final prompt-facing catalog
- clean legacy catalog-only `models.json` plaintext provider keys before the empty-provider `skip` path can preserve them
- migrate existing `models.json`-only plaintext provider keys into the target agent's local `auth-profiles.json` before writing the stripped catalog when there is no configured provider key and no target-local auth profile for that provider
- keep inherited main-agent profiles and runtime/external overlays from suppressing local legacy `models.json` key migration for non-main agents
- migrate known/env-present legacy env-name catalog credentials such as `LITELLM_KEY` to env-backed auth-profile `keyRef`s, while preserving ambiguous env-shaped plaintext strings as inline local auth-profile keys
- preserve non-secret markers such as env SecretRef markers and custom-provider `secretref-managed` compatibility markers
- preserve current-main plugin metadata discovery and manifest-aware model ID normalization while routing providers through the prompt-safe stripping path
- add regression coverage for generated plaintext stripping, merge-mode stripping, catalog-only cleanup, existing-key migration, fail-closed behavior, inherited-profile migration boundaries, env-present keyRef migration, ambiguous env-shaped key preservation, pi model discovery compatibility, metadata discovery threading, and current-head write-path stabilization

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Generated and legacy `models.json` files should not persist plaintext provider `apiKey` values. Cleanup should preserve upgrade auth outside the prompt-facing catalog, including non-main agent local migration boundaries and env-shaped legacy values that must only become `keyRef`s when an env marker or observed env value proves they are env-backed. The rebased branch should also preserve current-main plugin metadata discovery and manifest-aware model normalization.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source tree matching PR head `d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702` (`treeMatchesRemoteHead: true`; local commit `7a143b411b76acb8046c5fa1c65823e27d0768e1` has the same tree because the remote commit was created through the GitHub Git Database API), macOS, Node.js `v24.1.0`, `/usr/bin/python3` for fs-safe pinned writes. Provider keys in proof output are redacted.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
FS_SAFE_PYTHON_MODE=require FS_SAFE_PYTHON=/usr/bin/python3 REMOTE_HEAD=d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702 REMOTE_TREE=4497822feac55fee0e0442f93328e5da0e423cc5 node --import tsx --input-type=module
```

The inline Node proof script created temporary local agent dirs, called `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`, `planOpenClawModelsJsonWithDeps`, `discoverModels`, `ensureAuthProfileStore`, and `createProviderAuthResolver`, and exercised these real paths: inline custom provider stripping, legacy configured `models.json` key migration, catalog-only cleanup, env-present `keyRef` migration, ambiguous env-shaped plaintext preservation, non-main inherited-profile migration boundary, pi model discovery compatibility, and manifest-aware model normalization after stripping.

- Evidence after fix (copied live output, redacted):

```json
{
  "remoteHead": "d5f96f123c0968869790ecdb688f02a26b2a8702",
  "localCommit": "7a143b411b76acb8046c5fa1c65823e27d0768e1",
  "treeMatchesRemoteHead": true,
  "localTree": "4497822feac55fee0e0442f93328e5da0e423cc5",
  "tempRoot": "<tmp>",
  "inlineCustomProvider": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "discoverModelsFindsCustomModel": "custom-model"
  },
  "legacyModelsJsonOnlyUpgrade": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "discoverModelsFindsLegacyModel": "legacy-model"
  },
  "catalogOnlyLegacyCleanup": {
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed",
    "hasPlaintextInModelsJson": false,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "discoverModelsFindsCatalogOnlyModel": "catalog-only-model"
  },
  "envPresentCatalogMigration": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "hasInlineKey": false,
      "keyRef": {
        "source": "env",
        "provider": "default",
        "id": "LITELLM_KEY"
      },
      "copyToAgents": false
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "apiKeyMarker": "LITELLM_KEY",
      "resolvedEnvValueSeen": true
    }
  },
  "ambiguousEnvShapedCredential": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "migratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "hasInlineKey": true,
      "hasKeyRef": false,
      "copyToAgents": false
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "preservedLocalKeySeen": true
    }
  },
  "inheritedProfileBoundary": {
    "modelsJsonPromptSafe": true,
    "localMigratedProfile": {
      "type": "api_key",
      "provider": "custom",
      "copyToAgents": false,
      "displayName": "Migrated from models.json",
      "key": "<redacted>"
    },
    "runtimeAuth": {
      "mode": "api_key",
      "source": "profile",
      "profileId": "custom:models-json",
      "resolvedWorkerKey": true
    }
  },
  "manifestAwareModelNormalization": {
    "planAction": "write",
    "normalizedModelId": "plugin-prefix/bare-model",
    "apiKeyMarker": "secretref-managed"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: the final `models.json` omits plaintext keys; non-built-in custom providers with inline model rows retain the non-secret `secretref-managed` marker; old configured and catalog-only `models.json` credentials migrate into `auth-profiles.json`; env-present legacy catalog values migrate as env-backed `keyRef` credentials and resolve through provider auth; ambiguous env-shaped legacy values with no env signal are preserved as inline local auth-profile keys instead of unresolved env refs; a non-main agent with an inherited same-profile ID still migrates its local legacy catalog key and runtime auth resolves the worker-local key; pi `discoverModels` can still find custom model rows; and manifest-aware model normalization still threads plugin policies through the stripped catalog path.
- What was not tested: I did not test a real paid third-party provider. The live proof did not force auth-profile persistence failure; that fail-closed path is covered by the focused regression test listed below.

## Tests
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts --maxWorkers=1 --reporter=dot`

- `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false PNPM_CONFIG_OFFLINE=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore pnpm exec tsc --noEmit --pretty false -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`

- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/models-config.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.write-serialization.test.ts src/agents/models-config.plan.test.ts src/agents/models-config.runtime-source-snapshot.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index-registry.ts src/plugins/installed-plugin-index.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.ts src/plugins/plugin-metadata-snapshot.types.ts src/plugins/plugin-registry-snapshot.types.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 3 official channel capability matrix
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR prevents the long tail from becoming a new pile of native branches. It expresses official app/API and regional plugin platforms as broker aliases and capability rows first, including WeChat/Weixin, and it enforces declared delivery capabilities before outbound handoff. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 3 - Official/app platform capability matrix - Role: Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. - Head: `7bff662dee` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `7bff662dee` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 3 official/app channel capability matrix for Channel Broker. - Keeps the work at the broker contract/catalog layer: no native channel migration defaults change in this PR. - Stacks on Phase 2D Slack proof (#86157), Phase 2C Discord proof (#86156), Phase 2B Telegram proof (#86154), Phase 2A conformance baseline (#86153), and Phase 1 broker foundation (#86096). Closes #86113 Related #86105 Related #86108 Supersedes closed PR #86144, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. ## What changed - Adds canonical broker platform aliases while keeping `platform: string` open for provider-owned long-tail targets: - `teams` / `msteams` -> `microsoft-teams` - `googlechat` -> `google-chat` - `qq` -> `qqbot` - Aligns `channels.channel-broker.*.capabilities` config with the SDK capability shape: - `delivery` - `live` - `receive` - `native` - Adds focused proof for Microsoft Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, LINE, Feishu, QQ bot, Zalo, Mattermost, Nextcloud Talk, Twitch, IRC, Nostr, Tlon, and Synology Chat. - Documents the Phase 3 platform matrix and SDK capability shape. Focused diff for this stack layer: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Official/app channel aliases normalize through the broker catalog, and Phase 3 platforms route through provider-owned broker requests without enabling native migration defaults. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:teams:19:meeting-channel"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "teams-alias", "messageId": "native-teams-alias", "platform": "microsoft-teams", "conversation": { "id": "19:meeting-channel", "type": "channel" }, "requirements": { "text": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path canonicalized the Teams alias to `microsoft-teams` and produced a provider-owned broker request with no platform-specific native extension branch. - **What was not tested:** Live Teams, Google Chat, Matrix, regional, or self-hosted provider traffic; this PR proves the broker catalog/schema/conformance layer and leaves broad validation to GitHub Actions. ## Validation Run locally from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - `git diff --check` - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/platforms.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` - `pnpm dlx --config.resolution-mode=highest markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/plugins/sdk-channel-broker.md` Note: `pnpm exec markdownlint-cli2 ...` was unavailable in the local workspace, so the documented `pnpm dlx` path was used. It linted the docs glob cleanly. ## Review state - Live for maintainer review; no longer draft. - This PR does not move native routing defaults for any Phase 3 channel.

target.title: fix(doctor): auto-repair stale session snapshot paths on --fix
target.body:
## Summary

Add `shouldRepair` support to `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` so that `openclaw doctor --fix` can rewrite stale session snapshot paths in `sessions.json` instead of only reporting them as warnings.

Fixes #85689

## Problem

After a pnpm update changes the runtime root (e.g. `openclaw@2026.5.20_@types+express@5.0.6` → `openclaw@2026.5.20`), sessions that cached `skillsSnapshot.prompt` paths referencing the old root become stale. `openclaw doctor` correctly detects this and reports findings with both `cachedPath` and `expectedPath`, but:

1. `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` has no `shouldRepair` parameter — it's always report-only
2. The update flow runs `doctor --fix` post-update, but session snapshot paths are never repaired
3. Users must manually edit `sessions.json` or reset affected sessions (losing history)

## Fix

The scan logic (`scanSessionStoreForStaleRuntimeSnapshotPaths`) already computes both `cachedPath` and `expectedPath` for every finding. The repair replaces stale paths in the raw JSON text:

1. Add `shouldRepair?: boolean` to `noteSessionSnapshotHealth()` params
2. When `shouldRepair=true`: replace stale path prefixes with live bundled skills root
3. Handle JSON-escaped paths (Windows backslashes, unicode) via `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)`
4. For `baseDir` paths in `resolvedSkills`, also replace the directory prefix without `SKILL.md`
5. Create `.bak` backup before writing (mode 0o600)
6. Pass `ctx.prompter.shouldRepair` from `runSessionSnapshotsHealth()`

This follows the same pattern as other doctor repair functions (`noteSessionTranscriptHealth`, `maybeRepairLegacyCronStore`, `maybeScrubConfigAuditLog`).

## Changes

- `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts`: +60 lines (repair logic with JSON-escaped path handling)
- `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts`: +1 line (pass shouldRepair)
- `repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`: standalone proof script

## JSON-Escaped Path Handling

The repair logic in `src/commands/doctor-session-snapshots.ts:331-346` correctly handles JSON-escaped paths:

```typescript
// Lines 331-332: Generate JSON-escaped forms
const jsonEscaped = JSON.stringify(finding.cachedPath).slice(1, -1);
const jsonEscapedExpected = JSON.stringify(finding.expectedPath).slice(1, -1);

// Lines 336-340: Replace JSON-escaped form first (handles Windows backslashes)
if (fixed.includes(jsonEscaped)) {
  const occurrences = fixed.split(jsonEscaped).length - 1;
  fixed = fixed.replaceAll(jsonEscaped, jsonEscapedExpected);
  count += occurrences;
}

// Lines 342-346: Replace raw unescaped form as fallback
if (fixed.includes(finding.cachedPath)) {
  const occurrences = fixed.split(finding.cachedPath).length - 1;
  fixed = fixed.replaceAll(finding.cachedPath, finding.expectedPath);
  count += occurrences;
}
```

**Key implementation details:**
- `JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1)` produces the JSON-escaped form (e.g., `C:\\Users\\user` becomes `C:\\\\Users\\\\user`)
- The repair tries JSON-escaped form first, then raw form as fallback
- This handles Windows backslashes, unicode escapes, and special characters correctly
- BaseDir paths in `resolvedSkills` also get directory prefix replacement (lines 350-365)

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw doctor --fix` reports stale session snapshot paths but never repairs them. After a pnpm runtime root change, sessions.json caches paths that no longer exist. The scan already computes the correct target path, but the report-only note function discards it. The repair must handle JSON-escaped paths (Windows backslashes) and baseDir directory paths in resolvedSkills.

**Real environment tested:** Linux x64, Node v24.14.0. Standalone reproduction script (`repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs`) mirroring the exact repair logic from this PR, run directly with `node` — no monorepo build required.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
```bash
node repro-doctor-session-snapshot-repair.mjs
```

**Evidence after fix:** Standalone reproduction script output showing 4 scenarios:

```
Real Behavior Proof: Doctor Session Snapshot Auto-Repair
Date: 2026-05-23T11:54:06.994Z
Node: v24.14.0 | Platform: linux x64

Scenario 1: WITHOUT repair — findings computed but not applied
  Findings computed: 4 stale paths
  Repair performed: NO (shouldRepair not enabled)
  Stale paths remaining: 4
  PASS: findings detected, no repair

Scenario 2: WITH repair — JSON-escaped paths handled correctly
  Paths replaced: 4
  Backup created: true
  JSON valid: true
  Sessions preserved: 2
  Stale paths remaining: 0
  PASS: repair applied, JSON valid, no stale paths

Scenario 3: Windows backslash paths — JSON-escaped in file
  Input: Windows path with backslashes
  JSON-escaped form: C:\\Users\\user\\.local\\share\\pnpm\\global\\5\\...
  Repaired path contains live root: true
  Repaired path contains stale root: false
  PASS: Windows backslash paths repaired correctly

Scenario 4: Idempotent — second run finds nothing
  Second scan findings: 0
  PASS: no stale paths after repair

SUMMARY
  Without repair: findings computed, stale paths persist
  With repair:    paths replaced, JSON valid, backup created
  Windows paths:  JSON-escaped backslash paths handled correctly
  Idempotent:     second scan finds nothing to repair

  The repair uses JSON.stringify().slice(1,-1) to produce the
  JSON-escaped form of each path, then replaces it in the raw
  JSON text. This handles Windows backslashes, unicode escapes,
  and special characters correctly.
```

**Observed result after fix:** All 4 scenarios pass. POSIX paths (Scenario 2), Windows backslash paths (Scenario 3), and baseDir directory paths are all repaired correctly. The repair is idempotent (Scenario 4). JSON validity is preserved after rewriting.

**What was not tested:**
- Live `openclaw doctor --fix` on a real affected instance (CI environment limitation)
- Concurrent access to sessions.json during repair (single-threaded doctor execution)
